30.reason: [Off-by-one error: after reading a non-letter character at position i, the loop exits, and the null terminator should be placed at index i, not i+1. This causes an uninitialized byte to be included in the string and may lead to buffer overflow if i reaches max_word_len-1.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Off-by-one error: after reading a non-letter character at position i, the loop exits, and the null terminator should be placed at index i, not i+1. This causes an uninitialized byte to be included in the string and may lead to buffer overflow if i reaches max_word_len-1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be word_tem[i]='\0'; because the loop exits when word_tem[i] is not a letter, so valid letters are from 0 to i-1. Setting i+1 overwrites one extra byte and leaves word_tem[i] uninitialized (non-null), leading to garbage in the string.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Should be word_tem[i] = '\0'; because the loop exits when word_tem[i] is not a letter, so the valid letters are from 0 to i-1, and null terminator should be at index i.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be word_tem[i]='\0'; because the loop exits when word_tem[i] is non-letter, so the valid characters are from 0 to i-1, and null terminator should be at i.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Off-by-one error: after reading a non-letter character at position i, the loop exits, and the null terminator should be placed at word_tem[i], not i+1. This causes an uninitialized byte to be included in the string and may lead to buffer overflow if i reaches max_word_len-1.]

49.reason: [Incorrect arguments to qsort: first argument should be 'library', not 'library[0].ch'; third argument should be 'sizeof(struct word)', not 'sizeof(library[0])' (though that part is actually correct). The main issue is passing a char* instead of the array base address, causing undefined behavior.]

28.reason: [This may read beyond the buffer 'word_tem' if a word is longer than max_word_len-1, causing buffer overflow. Also, after reading a non-letter, it's stored in word_tem[i], but then word_tem[i+1] is set to '\0', which skips that character without reprocessing it.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This may read beyond the buffer 'word_tem' if a word is longer than max_word_len-1, causing buffer overflow. Also, after reading a non-letter, it's stored in word_tem[i], but then word_tem[i+1] is set to '\0', which skips that character without reprocessing it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [This reads one character beyond the last letter of the word, but the loop condition checks word_tem[i] before it's assigned, leading to reading uninitialized memory on the first iteration of the inner loop. Also, after reading a non-letter, it's not pushed back, so the next word may miss the first character after a delimiter.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [This may read beyond the bounds of word_tem if a word is longer than max_word_len-1, causing buffer overflow.]

31.reason: [This loop runs from 0 to i inclusive, but only indices 0 to i-1 contain letters; index i contains a non-letter (which caused loop exit), and i+1 was set to '\0'. So this may convert a non-letter to lowercase or access out-of-bound if i == max_word_len - 1.]
